name: Tetanus (Lockjaw)
description: An acute, often fatal bacterial disease caused by Clostridium tetani spores contaminating puncture wounds, deep cuts, or the umbilical stump in unhygienic deliveries. The bacteria release tetanospasmin, a potent neurotoxin that blocks inhibitory neurotransmitters, leading to severe lockjaw, neck stiffness, and painful generalized muscle spasms.

climateLinks.impact: Flooding events distribute tetanus spores from farm soils into wider walking environments, rising infection risks for barefoot rural populations.

prevention: Routine childhood immunization series with Tetanus Toxoid (TT)
Hygienic umbilical cord care and clean deliveries
Immediate surgical debridement and cleaning of contaminated soil wounds

climateThreatSchema.advisory: TETANUS ALERT: Initiate immediate wound cleaning with hydrogen peroxide or sterile saline at outposts for all agricultural puncture injuries.

triageAdvisory: A clinical emergency marked by masseter lockjaw or painful opisthotonos spasms. Administer Tetanus Immunoglobulin (TIG) and Tetanus Toxoid booster immediately. Keep patient in a dark, quiet room to prevent spasm-inducing sensory overloads.
